description:
You are a platform engineer reviewing nightly CI logs for two micro-services that run in a distributed staging environment.  
    A log-collection step has already placed yesterday’s raw container logs in  

        /home/user/ci/logs/  

    as the two files shown below.  
    Your job is to create a compact error-count report so that the next pipeline stage can decide whether to continue deployment.

    REQUIRED ACTION
    1. Read every line of every “*.log” file found under /home/user/ci/logs/ (there are exactly two of them at the moment).  
    2. Count how many lines in each file contain the literal substring «ERROR» (all-caps, ASCII, no surrounding brackets).  
    3. Produce the one-file report  

        /home/user/ci/summary/error_summary.txt  

       with the following precise format:  
       • One service per line.  
       • Each line must be:  

             <service-name>: <error-count>

         where  
         – <service-name> is the log file’s base-name without the “.log” extension.  
         – <error-count> is the integer number of «ERROR» lines you found in that file.  
       • Lines must be sorted alphabetically by <service-name>.  
       • End the file with a single trailing newline character.  

    OUTPUT VERIFICATION
    The automated grader will:  
    • Confirm that /home/user/ci/summary/error_summary.txt exists.  
    • Read the file and check that its contents exactly match the pattern above, including line order, colon, single space after the colon, and trailing newline.  
    • Re-compute the «ERROR» counts from the original logs and compare them with the numbers you wrote.

    CONSTRAINTS
    • You do NOT need root privileges.  
    • Solve the task using any standard GNU/Linux user-level utilities you prefer (e.g., grep, wc, awk, etc.).  
    • The complete solution can be done in as few as 2–3 shell commands.  
    • Do not manually edit the existing log files.

    When you are finished, you may print the contents of /home/user/ci/summary/error_summary.txt to the console so a human reviewer can see the result.
truth:
<!--
    Files and directories that already exist before the agent starts
    ---------------------------------------------------------------
    -->
    <pre_existing>
        <!-- Base directory -->
        /home/user/ci/
        <!-- Sub-directories -->
        /home/user/ci/logs/
    </pre_existing>

    <!--
    Pre-populated log files
    ---------------------------------------------------------------
    -->
    <file path="/home/user/ci/logs/service-auth.log"><![CDATA[
[2023-07-15T10:05:23Z] INFO Starting auth service
[2023-07-15T10:05:24Z] ERROR Failed to connect to DB
[2023-07-15T10:05:25Z] INFO Retrying connection
[2023-07-15T10:05:26Z] ERROR Failed to connect to DB
[2023-07-15T10:05:27Z] INFO Service shutting down
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/ci/logs/service-payment.log"><![CDATA[
2023-07-15 10:06:11 INFO  Payment processor started
2023-07-15 10:06:12 ERROR Gateway timeout
2023-07-15 10:06:13 ERROR Gateway timeout
2023-07-15 10:06:14 INFO  Payment retried OK
]]></file>

    <!--
    File that must be created by the agent
    ---------------------------------------------------------------
    -->
    <expected_output path="/home/user/ci/summary/error_summary.txt"><![CDATA[
service-auth: 2
service-payment: 2
]]></expected_output>
